intro: The mist rolls over the ancient glen...
Listen to the distant hum of the pipes...
The scent of heather fills the morning air...
Far across the sea to the Isle of Skye...
A lone fiddle plays a song of old...
The mountains stand as silent guards...
Beneath the grey skies of the North...
A fire crackles in the hearth of the croft...
verse: I walked the path where the wild thistle grows
The cold North wind carries the salt of the sea
By the banks of the Loch, the spirits reside
With a heavy heart, I leave the hills behind
My kinsmen fought where the heather turns red
The shadows lengthen on the cobblestone street
I remember the laughter in the golden barley fields
A silver moon rises over the jagged peaks
From the Lowland plains to the Highland peaks
My soul is bound to this rugged ground
We shared a dram by the peat-fire light
The eagle soars above the mist-covered crags
Every stone tells a story of a thousand years
Though I wander far, my roots remain deep
The roar of the Atlantic beats against the shore
The stag stands proud in the morning light
Wandering through the ruins of a forgotten keep
Writing letters home in the candlelight
The tartan colors fade but the spirit holds true
Crossing the moor as the storm begins to brew
pre_chorus: I hear the calling of the mountain stream
The blood of the clans flows through my veins
The pipes are sounding the gatherin' cry
Through the fire and the rain, we'll stand tall
It's a long road home over the rolling hills
Can you feel the magic of the Celtic soul?
chorus: Oh, Scotland, my home, where the wild hearts roam!
For the glory of the Highlands, we'll sing once more!
Across the sea, I'll return to thee!
Keep the fire burning till the break of day!
Stand together, brothers, for the land we love!
May the blessings of the hills be with you evermore!
The spirit of the North will never fade away!
Raise your voice for the brave and the free!
In the land of the mist, our legends live on!
From the islands to the glens, we are one!
bridge: Though oceans divide us and years pass us by
The memory of the rowan tree never shall die
I close my eyes and I'm back in the glen
To walk the heather with my brothers again
The ancient songs whisper through the pines
A sacred bond that time cannot untwine
drop: (The bagpipes break into a thunderous reel)
(The drums of the Highlands echo through the valley)
(A frantic fiddle solo ignites the air)
(The sound of clashing swords and war cries)
(Ethereal Celtic whistles soar above the beat)
(Stomping feet and rhythmic handclaps build the energy)
outro: The sun sets behind the Western Isles...
Until we meet again on the bonnie banks...
Farewell to the hills, but not for long...
The pipes fade into the evening mist...
Carry the song in your heart, wherever you go...
The Highlands are calling me home...
And the stars watch over the sleeping glen...
Scotland forever, in every breath I take.
